question: How do students join the Office Hours or live workshop sessions if the Zoom link isn’t public?
answer_agent:
Students don’t use a public Zoom link.

- The **Zoom link is only for instructors/presenters/TAs**
- **Students join via YouTube Live**
- Questions are submitted through **Slido** (the link is pinned in chat when live)
- The live video link is also posted in the **announcements channel on Telegram and Slack** before the session starts
- You can also watch on the **DataTalksClub YouTube channel**

So if you’re a student, the usual way in is **YouTube Live + Slido**, not Zoom.
answer_orig:
The zoom link is only published to instructors/presenters/TAs.

Students participate via YouTube Live and submit questions to Slido (link is pinned in the chat when live). The video URL should be posted in the [announcements channel on Telegram and Slack](https://t.me/dezoomcamp) before it begins. You can also watch live on the DataTalksClub [YouTube Channel](https://www.youtube.com/c/DataTalksClub).

Don’t post questions in chat as they may be missed if the room is very active.

question: Where should I begin if I just joined the LLM Zoomcamp, and what pages do I need to check first?
answer_agent:
If you just joined LLM Zoomcamp, the best place to start is the **main GitHub repository**:

- **LLM Zoomcamp GitHub repo**: https://github.com/DataTalksClub/llm-zoomcamp

From there, open the **lesson folders**. Each lesson page has the relevant videos linked at the top.

You should also check these pages first:

- **Course management platform** for syllabus, homework, deadlines, and progress:  
  https://courses.datatalks.club/llm-zoomcamp-2026/
- **Telegram channel** for announcements:  
  https://t.me/llm_zoomcamp

In short: **start with the GitHub repo**, then use the **course platform** to track the course and the **Telegram channel** for updates.
answer_orig:
Start with the [LLM Zoomcamp docs](https://datatalks.club/docs/courses/llm-zoomcamp/), the [general Zoomcamp logistics docs](https://datatalks.club/docs/courses/zoomcamp-logistics/), and the [LLM Zoomcamp GitHub repository](https://github.com/DataTalksClub/llm-zoomcamp).

You can start whenever you want. The videos and GitHub materials are available, and the deadlines are listed in the [course management platform](https://courses.datatalks.club/llm-zoomcamp-2026/).

A typical workflow is:

1. Watch the lesson videos.
2. Work through the lesson notebooks/code.
3. Read the homework instructions on GitHub.
4. Submit answers through the course platform before the deadline.

Homework is similar to the lesson flow, but uses a different dataset or slightly different task.

question: I can’t find my name on the leaderboard — how do I locate my own entry?
answer_agent:
Click **“Jump to your record on the leaderboard”** to locate your own entry.

A couple of related notes:
- When you first set up your account, you’re assigned a random display name, like **“Lucid Elbakyan.”**
- If you want to see or change your display name, go to **“Edit Course Profile.”**

There are two fields there:
- **First field:** your public nickname/display name for the leaderboard
- **Second field:** your official name for the certificate

If you want, I can also explain how to change your leaderboard name.
answer_orig:
When you set up your account, you are automatically assigned a random name, such as “Lucid Elbakyan.” Click on the "Jump to your record on the leaderboard" link to find your entry.

If you want to see what your Display name is, click on the "Edit Course Profile" button.

<{IMAGE:image_1}>

- **First field:** This is your nickname/displayed name. You can change it if you want to be known by your Slack username, GitHub username, or any other nickname of your choice. This is useful if you want to remain anonymous.
- **Second field:** Change this to your official name as in your identification documents—passport, national ID card, driver's license, etc. This is mandatory if you do not want "Lucid Elbakyan" on your certificate. This name will appear on your Certificate!

question: What’s the difference between the nickname shown publicly and the official name for the certificate?
answer_agent:
The two fields serve different purposes:

- **Public nickname/display name**: shown on the **leaderboard** and public course records. It can be an anonymous or chosen nickname.
- **Official name**: printed on your **certificate** and should match your identification documents.

So, if you want privacy publicly, use a nickname in the first field and your real legal name in the second field for the certificate.

question: Why does the homework content keep changing before it's released?
answer_agent:
Because the homework may still be in draft form. If the homework title contains `[DRAFT]`, it means it is not ready yet, so the content can still change.

The homework is considered ready only when both are true:
- the homework form is open on the course management platform, and
- the homework title does not contain `[DRAFT]`.

Until then, you should treat any work on it as provisional, since the final version may be different.
